summary: Stage decomp/matching PR touching the remaining gr work across grpushon, grpura, grgreens, headers, types, and one GALE01 symbol split. Bot report shows +1960 matched-code bytes, +32 matched-data bytes, 8 new matches, 17 unmatched-item improvements, and 2 grgreens unmatched-item regressions.

key_files.role: Largest visible decomp body update: implemented or improved multiple Pura stage functions, added debug/assert and camera type includes, replaced raw M2C_FIELD table access with typed grPu_803E6C0C entries, used HSD_ASSERT, and added noinline wrapper helpers for grPura_80211E08 call shaping.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grpura.c hunks for grPura_80211D00, grPura_802120E0, grPura_8021231C, grPura_802125F0, grPura_80212CD4, grPura_80212EF4, grPura_80212FC0, grPura_80213030
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report new matches/improvements for main/melee/gr/grpura

key_files.role: Large stage decomp update; loaded diff excerpt is truncated before this file, but metadata and bot report show significant work and one new matched function.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: bot report fn_802192A4 new match and grPushOn_80218888 improvement

key_files.role: Greens matching refinements: added/split grGr_803E787C table, changed getBlock indexing expression, rewrote bomb status setup into explicit BOMB calls, adjusted table selection and block-index order, replaced raw block-pointer math with getBlock calls in a swap loop, and changed row weight switch ordering.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grgreens.c hunks for grGr_803E787C, getBlock, grGreens_80214B58, grGreens_80214FA8, grGreens_802159B8, grGreens_80215D54, grGreens_802166C4
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report grgreens fn_80216DE4 new match and several grgreens improvements/regressions

key_files.role: Header surface changed for Pura stage declarations or types; exact hunk not included in loaded diff excerpt.
Header surface changed for PushOn stage; exact hunk not included in loaded diff excerpt.
Ground/stage type definitions changed, likely supporting the gr stage body work; exact hunk not included in loaded diff excerpt.
Adjusted grGreens .data symbol sizes by splitting grGr_803E787C from one 0xC4 object into 0x3C plus new grGr_803E78B8 size 0x88.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt grGr_803E787C size 0xC4 -> 0x3C and grGr_803E78B8 added size 0x88

what_changed.text: PR changed 7 files: grpura.c, grpushon.c, grgreens.c, grpura.h, grpushon.h, types.h, and config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
Bot report recorded 8 new matches: grpushon fn_802192A4; grpura grPura_80212EF4, grPura_8021231C, .sdata, grPura_80212FC0, grPura_80213030, grPura_80211D00; and grgreens fn_80216DE4.

Bot report recorded notable unmatched improvements including grPushOn_80218888 to 92.87%, grGreens_80214B58 to 99.26%, grPura_80212CD4 to 69.10%, grPura_802120E0 to 56.17%, and grPura_80213250 to 83.56%.
Bot report also recorded regressions in two unmatched grgreens items: grGreens_80215ED8 -12 bytes and fn_80215B84 -6 bytes.
grgreens changed block grid access from flat i * 6 + j indexing to a typed 2D-array cast in getBlock, and later used getBlock to replace explicit pointer/offset block swaps.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grgreens.c getBlock and grGreens_80215D54 hunks

what_changed.text: grpura changed table access in grPura_80212FC0 and grPura_80213030 from u16 pointer stepping and M2C_FIELD offsets to array-of-struct field access on grPu_803E6C0C.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grpura.c grPura_80212FC0 and grPura_80213030 hunks

smart_moves.text: Used typed helper or struct access to make recovered data layout explicit: grgreens getBlock gained a 2D cast and grpura table loops moved away from M2C_FIELD/raw pointer stepping.
Mapped raw __assert checks in grPura_802125F0 to HSD_ASSERT after adding baselib/debug.h.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grpura.c include baselib/debug.h and grPura_802125F0 assert hunk

smart_moves.text: Used a local helper BOMB to express repeated grGreens bomb-state assignment while preserving explicit call order at each index.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grgreens.c BOMB helper and grGreens_80214B58 hunk

smart_moves.text: Updated symbol metadata when a .data object boundary changed, rather than only compensating in C.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt grGr_803E787C split and grGr_803E78B8 addition

decomp_lessons.lesson: When repeated accesses target a known grid or table, recovering a typed indexing helper can improve readability and reduce raw pointer arithmetic, but row/column order must be checked carefully against call sites.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grgreens.c getBlock changed to typed 2D array cast
raw_diff: grgreens.c grGreens_802159B8 changes getBlock(gp, i, j) to getBlock(gp, j, i)
raw_diff: grgreens.c grGreens_80215D54 replaces offset math with getBlock

decomp_lessons.lesson: Replacing M2C_FIELD and pointer stepping with recovered struct fields can turn table-walking decomp into reviewable source while still supporting matching progress.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: bot report grPura_80212FC0 and grPura_80213030 new matches

decomp_lessons.lesson: Plain assert expansions in recovered stage code can often be restored to HSD_ASSERT when file/line evidence is present and the debug header is included.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grpura.c grPura_802125F0 __assert to HSD_ASSERT at 0x291 and 0x292

decomp_lessons.lesson: Data symbol boundaries may need to be corrected alongside code work when a recovered table split is represented in C.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt grGr_803E787C size reduction and grGr_803E78B8 addition
raw_diff: grgreens.c adds static s16 grGr_803E787C[30]

naming_conventions.text: Address-style function names such as grPura_802120E0, grPura_80212CD4, grGreens_80214B58, and fn_80216DE4 were retained where semantic names are not evidenced.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: function names in grgreens.c and grpura.c

naming_conventions.text: Short local role names were used where roles are clear, including gp, gobj, jobj, child, node, vec, quat, and row/col-style loop variables.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grpura.c grPura_8021231C and grPura_80213250 hunks
raw_diff: grgreens.c grGreens_80215D54 and grGreens_802166C4 hunks

naming_conventions.text: Some temporary or generated-looking names remain, such as uVar1, uVar3, uVar6, spilC, thingy, and var_r30; the slice does not include review discussion about whether these should be cleaned up.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Inserted grPura_80211E08_noinline and grPura_80211E08_noinline2 wrappers and routed a grPura_802125F0 call through the second wrapper, apparently to influence call/inlining shape; no review explanation is loaded.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grpura.c noinline wrapper additions and grPura_802125F0 call change

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used PAD_STACK(8) plus unused locals/padding in grPura_802120E0, indicating stack-shape matching tactics; the bot report shows the function improved but not matched.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grpura.c grPura_802120E0 includes UNUSED u8 _pad4[4] and PAD_STACK(8)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Changed ordinary constants from named f32 storage to inline float literals in grPura_80211D00 and grgreens extreme values, aligning with caution around literal/data ownership, though the PR evidence does not explain motivation.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grpura.c removes grPu_804DBA58/grPu_804DBA5C and uses 0.8f/3600.0f literals
raw_diff: grgreens.c replaces F32_MAX with 3.4028235e38f literals

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: In grGreens_80214B58, replaced a static const indices array plus loop with explicit BOMB(index) calls after zeroing the array in a for loop. This improved the bot score for that function to 99.26% but did not reach 100%.
In grPura_8021231C, used GET_JOBJ plus stack Vec3 and Quaternion temporaries for HSD_JObj transform copy; bot report says this function reached 100%.

review_feedback.text: No human review comments or reviews are present in the loaded slice.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl contains only empty PR body and decomp-dev[bot] issue comment

review_feedback.text: Automated report feedback: matched code increased by +0.05% (+1960 bytes), matched data increased by +32 bytes, with 8 new matches, 17 improvements, and 2 grgreens unmatched-item regressions.

follow_up_queries.reason: Loaded diff is truncated before grpushon.c, but bot report shows major progress there.
Headers/types changed but exact loaded hunks are unavailable; curator may need details before extracting header lessons.
Wrapper tactic is visible but not explained; needs verification before becoming a standard lesson.
Bot report shows two regressions; loaded slice does not show cause or resolution.

follow_up_queries.query: grPu_803E6C0C typed struct fields x00 x02 x04 x08
follow_up_queries.reason: Typed-field recovery is visible in body changes but exact type definition hunk is not loaded.

notes: No human review comments are loaded.
Raw diff is truncated, so grpushon.c and header/type details are only evidenced by changed-file metadata and bot report, not by visible hunks.
Bot report provides match/improvement/regression evidence but not local build commands, objdiff screenshots, or author rationale.
The PR is marked merged in metadata, but merge does not by itself prove each visible tactic is a reusable standard.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use typed table/grid access instead of M2C_FIELD or raw pointer stepping when the layout is recovered
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2534 shows stage code becoming more reviewable by moving grgreens block access through a typed 2D getBlock helper and moving grpura grPu_803E6C0C loops from pointer stepping/M2C_FIELD to named table fields. Bot evidence shows related functions reached or improved toward matches.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: grPura_80212FC0 and grPura_80213030 new matches

accepted_candidate_records.title: Noinline wrapper chains can appear as narrow call-shape tactics but require explicit evidence before standardization
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2534 adds grPura_80211E08_noinline and grPura_80211E08_noinline2 and changes a call in grPura_802125F0 through the wrappers. The slice supports recording this as an observed tactic, not as a recommended standard.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: grPura_802125F0 improved but not matched

source_update_candidates.title: src/melee/gr contains stage-specific ground decomp bodies and headers for Greens, Pura, and PushOn
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2534 changed src/melee/gr/grgreens.c, grpura.c, grpushon.c, corresponding headers, and gr/types.h while bot report classified units as main/melee/gr/*.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path-scope fact for curator review; do not promote from one PR without checking existing path facts.

source_update_candidates.title: Treat noinline wrapper chains as an exception requiring evidence
source_update_candidates.text: The PR visibly uses grPura_80211E08_noinline and grPura_80211E08_noinline2 to alter a call site. If this pattern recurs, standards may need an explicit note that noinline wrapper/helper chains are a narrow matching tactic like other codegen steering and require objdiff evidence and cleanup justification.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grpura.c noinline wrapper additions and use in grPura_802125F0
source_update_candidates.reason: Current standards mention manual inline expansion, dummy locals, volatile locals, PAD_STACK, pragmas, register, and asm, but this PR shows wrapper-based call-shape steering as a related tactic candidate.

source_update_candidates.title: config/GALE01/symbols.txt owns stage .data symbol boundary metadata
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2534 adjusted grgreens .data object sizes in config/GALE01/symbols.txt, splitting grGr_803E787C into size 0x3C and adding grGr_803E78B8 at size 0x88.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential source/path fact about where symbol-size boundary corrections are made; curator should compare with existing path facts.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all noinline wrapper chains are acceptable style; this slice lacks reviewer explanation and only shows an observed tactic.
Do not infer grpushon-specific lessons from the loaded diff excerpt; exact grpushon hunks are not present.
Do not claim the two grgreens regressions were accepted intentionally; the slice only includes the bot report and merge metadata.
Do not promote remaining generated-looking local names as a naming convention; they may be unresolved matching artifacts.
